cultural_significance: Shui Xian is a major Wuyi cultivar with centuries of history. Named after the narcissus flower ("Shui Xian") whose elegant form and fragrance early Fujian growers felt the tea resembled. The English rendering "Water Sprite" comes from a poetic translation of the flower’s name. "Lao Cong" designates tea made from old deep‑rooted bushes prized for mineral richness.
